message.content:
[
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "Periodic Table",
    "difficulty": "Easy",
    "questionText": "Which of the following elements has the highest ionization energy?",
    "optionA": "Sodium",
    "optionB": "Magnesium",
    "optionC": "Aluminum",
    "optionD": "Silicon",
    "correctAnswer": "B",
    "explanation": "Ionization energy increases across a period from left to right due to increasing nuclear charge. Among the given options, Magnesium is to the left of Aluminum and Silicon, but has a higher ionization energy than Sodium because of its stable electron configuration."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "s-block",
    "difficulty": "Easy",
    "questionText": "Which alkali metal has the lowest melting point?",
    "optionA": "Lithium",
    "optionB": "Sodium",
    "optionC": "Potassium",
    "optionD": "Cesium",
    "correctAnswer": "D",
    "explanation": "Cesium has the lowest melting point among the alkali metals due to its large atomic size and weak metallic bonding. The melting points decrease down the group as the atomic size increases and the metallic bond strength decreases."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "p-block",
    "difficulty": "Easy",
    "questionText": "Which of the following halogens is the strongest oxidizing agent?",
    "optionA": "Fluorine",
    "optionB": "Chlorine",
    "optionC": "Bromine",
    "optionD": "Iodine",
    "correctAnswer": "A",
    "explanation": "Fluorine is the strongest oxidizing agent among the halogens due to its highest electronegativity and smallest atomic size. The oxidizing power decreases down the group as the atomic size increases and the electron affinity decreases."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "d-block",
    "difficulty": "Easy",
    "questionText": "Which transition metal has the highest melting point?",
    "optionA": "Iron",
    "optionB": "Copper",
    "optionC": "Tungsten",
    "optionD": "Zinc",
    "correctAnswer": "C",
    "explanation": "Tungsten has the highest melting point among transition metals due to its strong metallic bonding resulting from the involvement of d-electrons. The melting points of transition metals are generally high but vary depending on the number of unpaired d-electrons."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "Coordination Compounds",
    "difficulty": "Easy",
    "questionText": "What is the coordination number of iron in [Fe(CN)6]4-?",
    "optionA": "2",
    "optionB": "4",
    "optionC": "6",
    "optionD": "8",
    "correctAnswer": "C",
    "explanation": "The coordination number is the number of donor atoms attached to the central metal ion. In [Fe(CN)6]4-, there are six cyanide (CN-) ions coordinated to the central Fe2+ ion, making the coordination number 6."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "Periodic Table",
    "difficulty": "Medium",
    "questionText": "Which of the following elements shows diagonal relationship with lithium?",
    "optionA": "Magnesium",
    "optionB": "Aluminum",
    "optionC": "Beryllium",
    "optionD": "Boron",
    "correctAnswer": "C",
    "explanation": "Beryllium shows a diagonal relationship with lithium due to similar polarizing power and size. Both elements form covalent compounds, have similar electronegativity, and their oxides and hydroxides are amphoteric."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "s-block",
    "difficulty": "Medium",
    "questionText": "Which alkali metal hydroxide is the strongest base?",
    "optionA": "LiOH",
    "optionB": "NaOH",
    "optionC": "KOH",
    "optionD": "CsOH",
    "correctAnswer": "D",
    "explanation": "CsOH is the strongest base among alkali metal hydroxides due to the largest size of Cs+ ion, which results in the weakest attraction for OH- ions. The basic strength increases down the group as the size of the cation increases."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "p-block",
    "difficulty": "Medium",
    "questionText": "Which of the following nitrogen oxides is paramagnetic?",
    "optionA": "N2O",
    "optionB": "NO",
    "optionC": "N2O3",
    "optionD": "N2O4",
    "correctAnswer": "B",
    "explanation": "NO (nitric oxide) is paramagnetic due to the presence of an unpaired electron in its molecular orbital configuration. The other nitrogen oxides mentioned are diamagnetic as they have all electrons paired."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "d-block",
    "difficulty": "Medium",
    "questionText": "Which transition metal ion has the maximum number of unpaired electrons in its +3 oxidation state?",
    "optionA": "Cr³⁺",
    "optionB": "Mn³⁺",
    "optionC": "Fe³⁺",
    "optionD": "Co³⁺",
    "correctAnswer": "B",
    "explanation": "Mn³⁺ has the maximum number of unpaired electrons (4) in its +3 oxidation state with electron configuration [Ar]3d4. Cr³⁺ has 3 unpaired electrons, Fe³⁺ has 5 unpaired electrons, and Co³⁺ has 4 unpaired electrons in high-spin configuration."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "Coordination Compounds",
    "difficulty": "Medium",
    "questionText": "What is the magnetic nature of [CoF6]3-?",
    "optionA": "Diamagnetic",
    "optionB": "Paramagnetic with 1 unpaired electron",
    "optionC": "Paramagnetic with 4 unpaired electrons",
    "optionD": "Paramagnetic with 6 unpaired electrons",
    "correctAnswer": "C",
    "explanation": "[CoF6]3- is paramagnetic with 4 unpaired electrons. Co³⁺ has d6 configuration and in the presence of weak field ligand F-, it forms a high-spin complex with 4 unpaired electrons."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "f-block",
    "difficulty": "Medium",
    "questionText": "Which lanthanide ion has the colorless aqueous solution?",
    "optionA": "La³⁺",
    "optionB": "Ce³⁺",
    "optionC": "Eu³⁺",
    "optionD": "Gd³⁺",
    "correctAnswer": "A",
    "explanation": "La³⁺ has a colorless aqueous solution because it has an empty f-subshell (4f0) and no f-f transitions are possible. The other ions have partially filled f-subshells and exhibit color due to f-f transitions."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "Metallurgy",
    "difficulty": "Hard",
    "questionText": "Which of the following processes is used for the extraction of aluminum from bauxite?",
    "optionA": "Smelting",
    "optionB": "Hall-Héroult process",
    "optionC": "Bessemer process",
    "optionD": "Cyanide process",
    "correctAnswer": "B",
    "explanation": "The Hall-Héroult process is used for the extraction of aluminum from purified bauxite (Al2O3). In this process, Al2O3 is dissolved in molten cryolite and electrolyzed to produce aluminum metal. Smelting is used for iron, Bessemer for steel, and cyanide for gold extraction."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "Environmental Chemistry",
    "difficulty": "Hard",
    "questionText": "Which of the following greenhouse gases has the highest global warming potential?",
    "optionA": "Carbon dioxide",
    "optionB": "Methane",
    "optionC": "Nitrous oxide",
    "optionD": "Sulfur hexafluoride",
    "correctAnswer": "D",
    "explanation": "Sulfur hexafluoride (SF6) has the highest global warming potential (23,900 times that of CO2 over 100 years) among the listed gases. While methane has a higher GWP than CO2, SF6 is extremely persistent and has a much higher radiative efficiency."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "d-block",
    "difficulty": "Hard",
    "questionText": "Which of the following complexes shows optical isomerism?",
    "optionA": [Co(NH3)4Cl2]+,
    "optionB": [Co(en)3]3+,
    "optionC": [CoF6]3-,
    "optionD": [Co(NH3)6]3+,
    "correctAnswer": "B",
    "explanation": "[Co(en)3]3+ shows optical isomerism because it has a chiral structure due to the presence of three bidentate ethylenediamine (en) ligands arranged in a propeller-like fashion. The other complexes either don't have chiral centers or have symmetrical arrangements that don't produce enantiomers."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "p-block",
    "difficulty": "Hard",
    "questionText": "Which of the following oxides of phosphorus is most acidic?",
    "optionA": P4O6",
    "optionB": P4O10",
    "optionC": HPO3",
    "optionD": H3PO4",
    "correctAnswer": "B",
    "explanation": "P4O10 (phosphorus pentoxide) is the most acidic oxide of phosphorus due to its higher oxidation state (+5) compared to P4O6 (+3). The acidity of oxides increases with increasing oxidation state of the central atom. P4O10 readily reacts with water to form phosphoric acid, which is a strong acid."
  },
  {
    "subject": "Chemistry",
    "topic": "Coordination Compounds",
    "difficulty": "Hard",
    "questionText": "Which of the following complexes will show geometrical isomerism?",
    "optionA": [Pt(NH3)4]2+",
    "optionB": [Pt(NH3)2Cl2]",
    "optionC": [PtCl6]2-",
    "optionD": [Pt(NH3)6]4+",
    "correctAnswer": "B",
    "explanation": "[Pt(NH3)2Cl2] will show geometrical isomerism (cis-trans isomerism) because it is a square planar complex with two different ligands. The cis isomer has adjacent ammonia ligands while the trans isomer has opposite ammonia ligands. The other complexes either have all identical ligands or don't have the necessary geometry for geometrical isomerism."
  }
]
